name: Gestao Hospital
description:
description='The project, titled "GestaoHospital," is a Hospital Management System designed to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of public health services in Brazil, specifically within the framework of the Sistema Único de Saúde (SUS). Its primary objective is to create an API that organizes and manages hospital resources, thereby improving patient care and resource allocation.

The system addresses the challenges faced by the SUS, which is one of the largest public health systems globally, by providing tools to manage hospital inventories, patient admissions, and resource distribution. Key functionalities include:

1. **Hospital Registration**: Allows for the registration of hospitals within the system.
2. **Patient Management**: Facilitates the check-in and check-out processes for patients, ensuring accurate tracking of patient status.
3. **Resource Allocation**: Enables hospitals to manage their inventories, including medical supplies and blood bank resources, ensuring that hospitals can efficiently respond to patient needs.
4. **Proximity Recommendations**: Utilizes patient location data to recommend the nearest hospitals with available beds, optimizing patient transfers and care.
5. **Inventory Management**: Provides functionalities to register and manage the stock of medical products and their quantities.

Overall, the GestaoHospital project aims to streamline hospital operations, reduce waste, and enhance the quality of healthcare services provided to the population.'
highLevelGoals: The Hospital Administrator aims to streamline hospital operations and resource management to improve overall efficiency and effectiveness in patient care.
The Healthcare Provider seeks to utilize the system to effectively manage patient admissions, track patient status, and ensure timely care delivery.
The Patient desires a system that facilitates quick access to medical services and ensures they are directed to the nearest available healthcare facilities.
The IT Administrator aims to maintain the system's functionality and reliability to support hospital operations without interruptions.
The Public Health Official seeks to utilize the system to oversee and evaluate the effectiveness of public health services and resource allocation.
The Supplier aims to efficiently manage inventory and ensure timely delivery of medical supplies to meet hospital needs.
All actors require the system to ensure the protection of sensitive patient and hospital data, complying with relevant privacy regulations.
The system aims to minimize resource wastage and improve the overall quality of healthcare services provided to the population.
lowLevelGoals: The Hospital Administrator can access real-time data on hospital operations to identify areas for improvement.
The Hospital Administrator can create detailed reports to analyze resource allocation and patient movement within the hospital.
The Healthcare Provider can input and update patient admission details to ensure accurate tracking of patient status.
The Healthcare Provider can monitor the current status and treatment of patients to ensure timely interventions.
The Patient can quickly find the nearest available healthcare services to receive timely medical attention.
The Patient can schedule appointments with healthcare providers to ensure timely access to medical services.
The IT Administrator can check the system's performance and reliability to ensure it meets operational standards.
The IT Administrator can apply necessary updates to the system to maintain security and performance.
The Public Health Official can retrieve data to evaluate the effectiveness of public health services.
The Public Health Official can create reports to assess how resources are allocated and the effectiveness of service delivery.
The Supplier can update and track inventory levels to ensure timely delivery of medical supplies to the hospital.
The Supplier can arrange for timely deliveries of medical supplies to ensure the hospital is adequately stocked.
All actors can ensure that sensitive patient data is encrypted to protect against unauthorized access.
All actors can define who has access to sensitive patient data to ensure compliance with privacy regulations.
All actors can review data on resource usage to find areas where waste can be reduced.
All actors can gather patient feedback to assess the quality of healthcare services provided.
The system can suggest nearby healthcare facilities based on the patient's current location to enhance access to medical services.
The Supplier can analyze inventory turnover to optimize stock levels and reduce waste.
